Main analysis

oh-my-codex (omx) residual cross-interest

Overview

This document provides a source-based analysis of remaining cross-cutting concerns that were not sufficiently addressed in previous dimension documents (based on [[00_omx_overview]]: CLI/setup, AGENTS.md contract, MCP, hooks, $-workflow, role prompt, Rust runtime, .omx/ state) in the oh-my-codex (OMX) codebase. The target of analysis is the actual source of /Users/jun/developer/codex/oh-my-codex: TypeScript src/ (40 modules), Rust crates/ (6), prompts/·skills/·templates/. The package version is 0.18.6 (package.json).

Architectural premise of OMX: OMX is a Codex CLI control plane without its own LLM loop. Therefore, while [[13_cc_etc]] (Claude Code)'s remaining concerns are focused on "runtime internal implementation" (provider SDK wrapping, 1P event logging, etc.), OMX's remaining concerns are focused on **the orchestration layer outside of Codex, which launches sessions, performs authentication, sends notifications, and manages the git worktree. This asymmetry itself is the biggest discovery.

This document covers the following topics: Git/VCS integration, model routing, error handling/recovery, telemetry/observability, authentication (multislot quota rotation), multimodal (imagegen), events/notifications, feature flags, packaging, testing, and i18n. items that do not exist in omx are clearly stated.

1. Git/VCS integration

OMX's Git integration is divided into two aspects: one that "injects safety rules into the LLM through a prompt" like [[13_cc_etc]], and the other where Rust/TS runtime directly spawns git and adjusts the work tree and rebase. The latter is unique to OMX.

1.1 Safety contract from the git-master prompt

prompts/git-master.md (role prompt) specifies the safety rules for the dedicated git agent as text (<scope_guard>):

- Work ALONE. Task tool and agent spawning are BLOCKED.
- Detect commit style first: analyze last 30 commits ...
- Never rebase main/master.
- Use --force-with-lease, never --force.
- Stash dirty files before rebasing.
- Plan files (.omx/plans/*.md) are READ-ONLY.

It is isomorphic to [[13_cc_etc]]'s "Git Safety Protocol" (NEVER push --force, NEVER reset --hard), but OMX isolates it into a single role prompt. Additionally, the commit style is first detected with git log -30 --pretty=format:"%s" and the process of adjusting the language (English/Korean) and format (semantic/plain) is forced (<explore>).

1.2 Team Worktree Isolation (src/team/worktree.ts)

$team workers separate git worktrees for each worker to work in parallel on the same repo without conflict. src/team/worktree.ts calls git directly as child_process:

work git call line
Repo root detection git rev-parse --show-toplevel 68
Branch name verification git check-ref-format --branch <name> 105
Check branch existence git show-ref --verify --quiet refs/heads/<name> 116
dirty decision git status --porcelain [--untracked-files=all] 124,137
Work tree organization git worktree prune 193
common git dir git rev-parse --git-common-dir 259
Add work tree git worktree add ... 456
Remove worktree git worktree remove --force <path> 510,548
Delete branch git branch -D <branch> 529

WorktreeMode is modeled as the sum of {enabled:false} / {enabled,detached,name}, and scope is 'launch' | 'team' | 'autoresearch' There are three. In other words, the work tree strategy is different for each single session, team, and research mode.

1.3 Team Operations Commit Hygiene (src/team/commit-hygiene.ts)

$team is not a simple fan-out, but a git state machine where the leader integrates worker branches. commit-hygiene.ts types eight operational commits (TeamOperationalCommitKind) and records each result in an append-only log (TeamOperationalCommitEntry):

TEAM_OPERATIONAL_COMMIT_KINDS:
  auto_checkpoint, integration_merge, integration_cherry_pick,
  cross_rebase, worker_clean_rebase, leader_integration_attempt,
  shutdown_checkpoint, shutdown_merge
상태: applied | noop | conflict | skipped

The classification of cross_rebase/integration_merge/cherry_pick makes the process of integrating worker output into the leader HEAD traceable. There is a separate test:team:cross-rebase-smoke regression suite in package.json.

1.4 commit Lore guard (src/config/commit-lore-guard.ts)

Commit guard turned on with the OMX_LORE_COMMIT_GUARD environment variable (or shell_environment_policy.set / env in config.toml). Referenced in the PostToolUse hook (src/scripts/codex-native-pre-post.ts), which applies the policy for "lore" (narrative/document) commits. Doctor also checks whether it is active (src/cli/doctor.ts).

Assessment: Git/VCS is the most mature remaining concern in OMX. While CC stays at "prompt safety rules + worktree detection", OMX orchestrates multi-worker integration directly into git worktree + 8 types of operational commit log.

2. Model routing / provider abstraction

OMX does not call LLM directly, so there is no [[13_cc_etc]] style "provider SDK wrapping (Bedrock/Vertex)". Instead, the routing layer**, which determines which models to pass to Codex, is the body of model routing (src/config/models.ts).

2.1 Three-lane model classification and analysis sequence

models.ts divides the model into three lanes and hardcodes the default values:

lane default constant env override
frontier DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL = 'gpt-5.5' OMX_DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL
standard DEFAULT_STANDARD_MODEL = 'gpt-5.4-mini' OMX_DEFAULT_STANDARD_MODEL
spark (low complexity) DEFAULT_SPARK_MODEL = 'gpt-5.3-codex-spark' OMX_DEFAULT_SPARK_MODEL / OMX_SPARK_MODEL
team child DEFAULT_TEAM_CHILD_MODEL = standard OMX_TEAM_CHILD_MODEL

The interpretation order is defined for each lane. Example: getMainDefaultModel() is OMX_DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL → model in config.toml → DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL. getModelForMode(mode) is models[mode] → models.default → main default in .omx-config.json. If there is no explicit override, the standard lane inherits frontier and maintains leader-worker model synchronization (models.ts:268).

2.2 Reasoning effort routing by agent

models.ts prefers tuning reasoning_effort over model forcing (consistent with [[00_omx_overview]] §6.2). The agentReasoning block in .omx-config.json maps effort by role to 'low'|'medium'|'high'|'xhigh' (getAgentReasoningOverride). The role name is normalized and verified as /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]*$/.

2.3 Codex provider env passthrough

readActiveProviderEnvOverrides() (models.ts:179) reads model_providers.<active>.env_key in Codex config.toml and passes only the corresponding env variables to the child process. In other words, OMX does not handle the provider authentication itself, but only relays the env_key declared by Codex — in contrast to CC's direct branching of the getProvider() expression.

flowchart TD
    Req["모델 요청 (mode/agent)"] --> ModeChk{".omx-config.json<br/>models[mode]?"}
    ModeChk -->|yes| Use1["모드별 모델"]
    ModeChk -->|no| DefKey{"models.default?"}
    DefKey -->|yes| Use2["default 모델"]
    DefKey -->|no| EnvChk{"OMX_DEFAULT_*<br/>env?"}
    EnvChk -->|yes| Use3["env 모델"]
    EnvChk -->|no| CfgChk{"config.toml<br/>model?"}
    CfgChk -->|yes| Use4["Codex config 모델"]
    CfgChk -->|no| HC["하드코딩 기본값<br/>(gpt-5.5 등)"]
    Use1 & Use2 & Use3 & Use4 & HC --> Effort["agentReasoning<br/>effort override"]
    Effort --> Codex["codex 자식 프로세스<br/>(--model / -c)"]

3. Error handling/recovery pattern

OMX's error handling is characterized by no central errors/ module (in contrast to CC) and a pattern of distributed "recovery recommendations" by concern.

3.1 PostToolUse error classification and advisory (codex-native-pre-post.ts)

src/scripts/codex-native-pre-post.ts analyzes the tool results and injects recommendations on what to do instead of retry. We distinguish three signals (lines 1235-1274):

signal trigger Advisory
MCP transport failure (McpTransportFailureSignal) MCP tool loses transport/server connection "Do not blindly retry, fall back to OMX CLI parity surface, preserve state"
Bash setup failed command not found / permission denied / missing file "Retry after verifying PATH, dependencies, permissions, and path"
Non-zero termination but useful output non-zero exit + informative stdout "Review and report the output and retry (do not simply conclude that it was a failure)"

Core design: Rather than running an automatic retry loop, OMX returns structured recovery instructions to the model as text. The key to avoiding single points of failure is to bypass the CLI/JSON parity of the same tool ([[00_omx_overview]] §3.1) when the MCP dies.

3.2 Quota error detection → authentication rotation (auth/quota-detector.ts)

The most sophisticated automatic recovery lies in the authentication layer (details §7). isQuotaError() pattern-matches Codex stderr/stdout/structuredError together:

const DEFAULT_PATTERNS = [/\b429\b/i, /\bquota\b/i, /rate\s*limit(?:ed|s)?/i, /too\s+many\s+requests/i];

User-defined quotaPatterns can also be added as regular expressions, and if regular expression compilation fails, it falls back to substring matching (quota-detector.ts:39-45). When a quota is detected, the slot rotation of §7 is activated.

3.3 Notifications HTTP client timeout/proxy (notifications/http-client.ts)

Notification transmission uses an independent HTTP client (src/notifications/http-client.ts). If globalThis.fetch exists, it falls back to AbortSignal.timeout(timeoutMs), and if not, it falls back to native http/https request (lines 155-165). Respects proxy env(HTTPS_PROXY/http_proxy/all_proxy) and allows only HTTP/HTTPS protocols. There is no exponential backoff retry like CC's OTLP retry layer, but instead cooldown/dedupro to prevent runaway (§5.3).

3.4 Spawn error classification (auth/hotswap.ts)

runCodexDirect() uses classifySpawnError() to classify codex execution failures as missing (not in PATH)/blocked (permission blocked) and creates a user-friendly message (hotswap.ts:84-92).

Evaluation: Although there is no central error module, the OMX-style distributed recovery of “recovery recommendation instead of retry + authentication rotation + notification cooldown” is suitable for the control plane nature.

4. Telemetry / Observability

OMX does not send data to external telemetry backends. 1P event logging like tengu_, remote collection like OpenTelemetry/OTLP, GrowthBook/Statsig does not exist at the source. Instead, all observability is implemented as local files + local HUD/Trace — a privacy/autonomy-first design choice.

4.1 Trace MCP — Local JSONL based (mcp/trace-server.ts)

The omx_trace server (src/mcp/trace-server.ts, omx-trace v0.1.0) provides timelines/stats by reading turns-*.jsonl (generated by notify hooks) from .omx/logs/:

trace_timeline   ← 턴별 input/output preview 시간순 정렬
trace_summary    ← 집계 통계
TraceEntry: { timestamp, type, thread_id?, turn_id?, input_preview?, output_preview? }

keepLastEntries() is a heap-like structure that maintains only the latest N entries in memory to withstand large logs (trace-server.ts:36). This is OMX's observable data model — querying local JSONL without sending it remotely.

4.2 HUD — Local state rendering

omx hud ([[00_omx_overview]] §7.4, src/hud/) renders the .omx/ state to the terminal. Adjust the observation level with the --watch (1s polling)/--json/--tmux/--preset flag. This is local/real-time observation, not a remote dashboard.

4.3 Comparison: Asymmetry with CC

observation dimension Claude Code oh-my-codex
1P event logging tengu_* Anthropic Collection None
3P OpenTelemetry OTLP grpc/http support None
metric counter claude_code.* None
local trace (limited) omx_trace JSONL + HUD
Event Notification (Slack check prompt) OpenClaw/Discord/Telegram/Slack push (§5)

In other words, OMX's "telemetry" is divided into observation (local trace/HUD) + notification (push), and remote collection for analysis is intentionally absent.

5. Event/Notification System

It is one of OMX's richest remaining interests. While CC only handles notifications as a prompt-level "Slack check", OMX has a full-fledged event system called multi-channel notifications + OpenClaw gateway (src/notifications/, src/openclaw/).

5.1 Multi-channel notifications (notifications/types.ts)

NotificationChannel supports 5 types (types.ts:29-33):

discord | discord-bot | telegram | slack | webhook

Each channel has a dedicated config (Discord webhookUrl, Discord Bot token+channelId, Telegram, Slack incoming webhook, generic webhook), and custom_webhook_command is normalized to the OpenClaw gateway (types.ts:150). The transport uses the proxy-aware HTTP client of §3.3.

5.2 OpenClaw Gateway — Hook events out (openclaw/)

src/openclaw/ is a bridge that wakes up Codex hook events to an external gateway. event enum(openclaw/types.ts:13):

session-start | session-end | session-idle | ask-user-question | stop

Each event is mapped to a gateway (HTTP or CLI command) and an instruction (OpenClawHookMapping). HTTP gateway requires HTTPS (only localhost accepts HTTP), default timeout 10s. The command gateway interpolates the {{variable}} template after shell escaping, and the timeout precision is clamped to gateway timeout > OMX_OPENCLAW_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS > 5000ms (types.ts). This is a human-in-the-loop channel where “the agent asks a question or pushes it to the person when it stops.”

5.3 Cooldown / Dedupe / Idle (event throttling)

Notification flood prevention is sophisticated:

  • dispatch-cooldown.ts: Minimum dispatch interval. OMX_DISPATCH_COOLDOWN_SECONDS (default 60s) > config, state file .omx/state/[sessions/<id>/]dispatch-notif-cooldown.json. If 0, it is inactive.
  • idle-cooldown.ts: Cooldown for idle notifications only.
  • lifecycle-dedupe.ts: Deduplication of lifecycle events.

This throttling layer is an OMX-like mechanism that corresponds to CC's "event sampling (shouldSampleEvent)" — except that the goal is not to reduce analysis data, but to reduce push to people.

5.4 reply-listener — Two-way notifications

notifications/reply-listener.ts (34KB, maximum file) is a listener that receives replies to notifications. It is tested live with the test:reply-listener:live script. In other words, OMX notifications are not one-way notifications, but are a two-way channel that injects the human response back into the session.

6. Multimodal (imagegen / visual)

While CC's multimodal is "image attachment input + screenshot output (sharp/resvg)", OMX's multimodal is a continuation that connects the Codex built-in image_gen across turn boundaries and a visual verification gate.

6.1 imagegen continuation (src/imagegen/continuation.ts)

When the Codex built-in image_gen call cannot complete within one turn, prepareImagegenContinuation() queues a Stop hook follow-up:

.omx/state/sessions/<id>/imagegen-pending.json  ← ImagegenContinuationRecord{version,id,artifact_name,after,status:"pending",resume_instruction}

resume_instruction is a specific instruction to "find the latest generated image in $CODEX_HOME/generated_images/<session> and copy it to the workspace → crop/post-process → visual QA/visual-verdict gate → update Ralph progress status and then continue" (continuation.ts:81-93). It is passed to the next Stop checkpoint with injectExecFollowup(). In other words, OMX Multimodal is a persistence pattern that prevents "completion of image creation means the end of the task".

6.2 visual-verdict gate (src/visual/)

src/visual/verdict.ts + constants.ts handles visual verification verdicts in the $visual-ralph/$visual-verdict workflow. Visual-verdict JSON is recorded in ralph-progress.json ([[00_omx_overview]] §8.1), so visual output is also subject to the verification-gate.

Notice of absence: OMX does not have its own image rendering, screenshot, or clipboard copy like CC's sharp/resvg.** Image creation is delegated to Codex's built-in image_gen, and OMX only owns continuation and verification.

7. Authentication / API Key Management — Multi-Slot Quota Rotation

The core of OMX authentication is not CC-style "token source priority/OAuth flow", but hotswap (src/auth/) that stores **multiple Codex accounts (auth.json) in slots and automatically switches when quota is exhausted. This is a powerful asset unique to OMX.

7.1 Slot storage (auth/storage.ts)

Store each account's auth.json as a named slot. AuthMetadata tracks lastUsedAt/lastQuotaAt/exhaustedAt per slot. Security is solid:

  • Atomic Write: atomicWriteFile() creates *.tmp-<pid>-<ts>-<rand> temporary file → rename (storage.ts:36).
  • Private Permissions: chmod with AUTH_FILE_MODE (type 0600), ensurePrivateDir.
  • Symbolic link blocking: assertNoSymlink() — Reject if auth target is a symlink (block attack vector).
  • Slot Name Validation: validateSlotName().

7.2 Rotation policy (auth/rotation.ts, config.ts)

AuthRotationMode = "round-robin" | "priority" | "manual" (default round-robin). The config is merged from the [omx.auth] table in .omx/config.toml as cwd → omx.toml → ~/.omx (config.ts:48). buildRotationPlan() creates the slot order for each mode, and nextSlotAfter() skips exhausted slots and selects the next.

7.3 hotswap loop (auth/hotswap.ts)

The core loop of omx auth --hotswap:

sequenceDiagram
    participant H as runAuthHotswap
    participant C as codex (child)
    participant S as slot storage
    H->>S: useSlot(slot0) → live auth.json
    H->>C: spawn codex (slot0)
    C-->>H: exit status
    alt status == 0
        H-->>H: return 0 (성공)
    else isQuotaError(stderr/429)
        H->>S: markSlotQuota(slot0)
        H->>S: findLatestRolloutSession() (resume 대상)
        H->>S: nextSlotAfter() → slot1
        H->>C: codex resume <sessionId> (preserved flags, slot1)
    else 기타 에러
        H-->>H: return status (비-쿼터는 즉시 종료)
    end
  • When a quota is detected, it is displayed with markSlotQuota, and findLatestRolloutSession() finds the interrupted Codex session and continues it with codex resume <id> (hotswap.ts:208). This means that account switching occurs without any loss of work.
  • buildResumeArgsWithPreservedFlags() preserves -c/--config/--model/-m/--dangerously-bypass-... and resumes (hotswap.ts:100).
  • If mode === "manual", the user is guided to omx auth use <slot> without automatic conversion.

7.4 시크릿 redaction (auth/redact.ts)

All codex stderr and error messages go through redactAuthSecrets() before hitting the model/log. 5종 패턴을 마스킹한다:

JSON_OAUTH_TOKEN_FIELD ("access_token"/"refresh_token"/"id_token" 값)
access/refresh/id_token 라인, bearer <...>, sk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,}, session/auth/api_token

It is narrower than CC's 25 secret patterns (OMX focuses on OAuth tokens and sk- keys), but the difference is that it is applied to all codex stderr streams in batches (hotswap.ts:80,215,219).

Evaluation: Multi-slot quota rotation + session resume is a unique feature of OMX that is not present in CC. “Exhaust quota → next account → resume same session” has direct value in long-term autonomous execution.

8. Feature flags

OMX에는 CC의 GrowthBook/Statsig 같은 원격 피처 플래그 시스템이 없다. 대신 두 종류의 로컬 플래그가 있다.

8.1 Codex 호환 피처 플래그 탐지 (config/codex-feature-flags.ts)

이것은 "OMX 기능 롤아웃"이 아니라 설치된 Codex CLI가 어떤 피처를 노출하는지 탐지하는 호환성 플래그다. [features].hooks(현행) vs [features].codex_hooks(레거시) 철자를 판별한다:

resolveCodexHookFeatureFlag():
  featureNames.has("hooks") → "hooks"
  featureNames.has("codex_hooks") → "codex_hooks"
  codexVersion >= 0.130.0 → "hooks"
  else fallback

Check version-based fallback with parseCodexCliVersion() + isCodexCliVersionAtLeast([0,130,0]) and plugin_hooks support with supportsCodexPluginScopedHooks(). In other words, OMX has a flag to adapt to Codex versions and features, but does not have its own A/B rollout flag.

8.2 Environment variable-based function gate

The remaining feature toggles are simple env variables: OMX_LORE_COMMIT_GUARD (§1.4), OMX_DISPATCH_COOLDOWN_SECONDS (§5.3), OMX_DEFAULT_*_MODEL (§2), OMX_OPENCLAW_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS (§5.2), and OMX_ROOT. This is a local deterministic setting, not a remote control.

9. Packaging/Distribution

OMX differs from CC (pure npm bundle) in that it is hybrid packaging (npm + Rust cargo-dist).

9.1 npm package (package.json)

"name": "oh-my-codex", "version": "0.18.6",
"bin": { "omx": "dist/cli/omx.js" }, "type": "module",
"engines": { "node": ">=20" },
"dependencies": { "@iarna/toml", "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk", "zod" }
  • Contrast with CC: has actual runtime dependencies (toml parser, MCP SDK, zod). CC had dependencies: {} zero bundle.
  • Include dist/, crates/, skills/, prompts/, templates/, plugins/, and src/scripts/ in files — Distribute TS output + Rust source + assets together.
  • postinstall: postinstall-bootstrap.js — Dynamically import dist/scripts/postinstall.js, but non-fatally skips (postinstall-bootstrap.js:16) if it is missing or fails. Installation robustness comes first.

9.2 Rust cross-compilation (dist-workspace.toml, Cargo.toml)

The six workspaces in crates/ (all v0.18.6) are built with cargo-dist 0.31.0. Target of dist-workspace.toml:

aarch64-apple-darwin, aarch64-unknown-linux-{gnu,musl},
x86_64-apple-darwin, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, x86_64-unknown-linux-{gnu,musl}

However, it specifies that installers = [] and "maintains a custom release.yml" — uses a manual release pipeline instead of cargo-dist's automatic workflow. RELEASE_PROTOCOL.md exists separately.

9.3 Build pipeline (package.json scripts)

build:full = build(tsc) + build:explore:release + build:sparkshell + build:api. prepack forces verify:native-agents + sync:plugin + verify:plugin-bundle + native asset cleanup. For the plugin mirror (plugins/oh-my-codex), SSOT synchronization is verified with sync-plugin-mirror.js --check.

10. Test infrastructure

The biggest difference from CC. CC is a decompiled bundle, so no tests were visible (N/A), but OMX is open source and exposes a vast test infrastructure.

10.1 scale

  • 346 *.test.ts files, 36 __tests__/ directories.
  • Runner: node --test (Node built-in) + c8 coverage. src/scripts/run-test-files.js runs dist/ recursively.
  • Rust: cargo test (e.g. cargo test -p omx-explore-harness).

10.2 Coverage Gate (package.json, COVERAGE.md)

coverage:team-critical forces lines≥78 / functions≥90 / branches≥70 / statements≥78 on dist/team · dist/state (--check-coverage). In other words, the team runtime has 90% function coverage as a CI gate.

COVERAGE.md maintains a feature matrix targeting >=90% parity compared to oh-my-claudecode (OMC) (Last Updated 2026-02-22, TOTAL ~95%). Example: Agent Definitions 100%, Code Intelligence ~58% (pragmatic 7/12 tools), Python REPL 0%.

10.3 Regression suite naming

Regression tests tied to specific bugs/PRs are isolated into scripts: test:recent-bug-regressions, test:team:cross-rebase-smoke, test:ralph-persistence, test:explicit-terminal-contract, test:compat:rust (verifies TS behavior equivalence with Rust binary with OMX_COMPAT_TARGET). This is evidence of the discipline of “leave a regression test for each bug.”

10.4 Smoke/Live Testing

smoke:packed-install (simulating a packed npm install), test:sparkshell, test:reply-listener:live (live testing of actual notification replies), demo-team-e2e.sh.

11. Internationalization / Accessibility

11.1 i18n — absent

There is no internationalization framework. All i18n/gettext/Intl./toLocaleString/localize/translate matches checked with grep are irrelevant — the only translate* functions are translatePathForMsys() (Windows MSYS path translation) in src/team/tmux-session.ts translateWorkerLaunchArgsForCli() (translate Worker CLI arguments), which is not a language translation but a path/argument conversion. All user-facing strings are hardcoded in English.

However, the git-master prompt detects the commit message language and adjusts it (English/Korean, §1.1) — multilingualization of the output is delegated to LLM.

11.2 Accessibility / Cross-platform

  • Windows partial support: translatePathForMsys, windowsHide (hotswap.ts:76), chmod process.platform !== "win32" guard (storage.ts:47). However, although there is windows-msvc in the Rust target of §9.2, omx-explore does not support Windows due to WINDOWS_UNSUPPORTED_ALLOWLIST_MESSAGE ([[00_omx_overview]] §7.3). tmux-dependent workflows also assume POSIX.
  • Output redaction: Secret masking (§7.4) is a security accessibility element.

12. Technical Reference

Main file path

area file
git-master safety agreement /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/prompts/git-master.md
Team Worktree Isolation /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/team/worktree.ts
Team Commit Hygiene /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/team/commit-hygiene.ts
commit lore guard /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/config/commit-lore-guard.ts
model routing /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/config/models.ts
Error advisory/transport failure /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/scripts/codex-native-pre-post.ts
Quota detection /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/auth/quota-detector.ts
authentication hotswap loop /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/auth/hotswap.ts
Slot storage (atomic/symlink defense) /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/auth/storage.ts
Rotation Policy /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/auth/rotation.ts
Secret redaction /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/auth/redact.ts
Notification type (5 channels) /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/notifications/types.ts
Notification HTTP Client /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/notifications/http-client.ts
Dispatch Cooldown /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/notifications/dispatch-cooldown.ts
OpenClaw Gateway /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/openclaw/{types,index}.ts
Trace MCP /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/mcp/trace-server.ts
imagegen continuation /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/imagegen/continuation.ts
visual-verdict /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/visual/verdict.ts
Codex feature flag detection /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/config/codex-feature-flags.ts
Packaging (Rust dist) /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/dist-workspace.toml
Coverage Matrix /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/COVERAGE.md

Environment variable reference

variable Use
OMX_DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL / _STANDARD_ / _SPARK_ Basic model for each lane
OMX_TEAM_CHILD_MODEL Tim Walker Model
OMX_LORE_COMMIT_GUARD lore commit guard enabled
OMX_DISPATCH_COOLDOWN_SECONDS Notification Dispatch Cooldown (default 60s)
OMX_OPENCLAW_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS OpenClaw command gateway timeout
OMX_ROOT .omx/ root override
CODEX_HOME / CODEX_SQLITE_HOME Codex Home (injected by hotswap)
HTTPS_PROXY / http_proxy / all_proxy Notification HTTP Proxy

Maturity Assessment

area Maturity Remarks
Git/VCS (worktree·commit hygiene) ★★★★★ Multiworker git orchestration
Model Routing (3-lane) ★★★★☆ env/config resolution chain, effort override
Error handling (distributed advisory) ★★★☆☆ No central module, focus on recovery recommendations
Telemetry (remote collection) ★☆☆☆☆ Remote Absence — Intentional
Observability (local trace/HUD) ★★★★☆ JSONL trace + real-time HUD
Event/Notification ★★★★★ 5 channels + OpenClaw + two-way reply
Certification (multi-slot rotation) ★★★★★ Automatic quota conversion + session resume
multimodal (imagegen) ★★★☆☆ continuation·verification only, rendering delegation
Feature flag ★★☆☆☆ Codex compatible detection + env, no remote rollout
packaging(npm+cargo-dist) ★★★★☆ Hybrid, cross-compilation
testing infrastructure ★★★★★ 346 test + coverage gate
i18n ★☆☆☆☆ Absent (English hardcoded)

cli-jaw comparison implications

  • (Multi-Slot Authentication Rotation Porting Value) "Quota detection (isQuotaError) → slot switching → aborted session resume" (hotswap.ts:183-209) in OMX src/auth/ is a proven pattern to bypass single-account quota limits in long-term autonomous execution. If [[cli-jaw]] suffers from the same limitations, this slot model, which includes atomic writes, symlink blocking, and 0600 permissions (storage.ts), is a direct reference — provided it also comes with the discipline of applying secret redactions (redact.ts) to all child stderr streams.

  • (absence of telemetry is a design choice) OMX had no remote telemetry (1P Events/OTLP/GrowthBook) and limited observations to local trace JSONL + HUD. If cli-jaw prioritizes privacy and autonomy, the OMX model of "remote collection 0 + local queryable JSONL" has less operational and trust burden than CC-type dual telemetry. However, when analysis and aggregation are necessary, an additional path to opt-in export of local traces must be designed.

  • (Notifications as first-class event channels) OMX views notifications not as simple notifications, but as a human-in-the-loop two-way channel (5 channels + OpenClaw ask-user-question/stop events + reply-listener response injection + cooldown/dedupe). If cli-jaw aims for autonomous execution that requires human intervention, designing a push channel + response reception + throttling (cooldown/dedupe/idle) as a set enables collaboration without congestion.

  • (Test/Coverage by contract) OMX has 346 tests, coverage:team-critical(functions≥90%) CI gates, bug-specific named regression suites (test:recent-bug-regressions/cross-rebase-smoke), and TS↔Rust equivalence verification (test:compat:rust). If cli-jaw has a multi-runtime (e.g. Node+Rust) structure, the pattern of nailing down operational equivalence through cross-implementation tests like OMX_COMPAT_TARGET is directly responsible for preventing regression.

Change history

  • 2026-05-29: First written based on oh-my-codex v0.18.6 source. Git/VCS (team/worktree.ts·commit-hygiene.ts·git-master.md), 3-lane model routing (config/models.ts), distributed error advisory (codex-native-pre-post.ts·quota-detector.ts), local observability (trace-server.ts·HUD, absence of remote telemetry), 5-channel notification + OpenClaw two-way event (notifications/·openclaw/), multi-slot quota rotation authentication (auth/hotswap.ts·storage.ts·rotation.ts·redact.ts), imagegen continuation multimodal (imagegen/continuation.ts·visual/), Codex compatible features Includes flags (codex-feature-flags.ts), npm+cargo-dist hybrid packaging, 346 test+coverage gate, i18n absence check. Analysis of asymmetry compared to CC (absence of remote telemetry vs. abundance of multi-slot authentication and multi-channel notifications).

## Korean corpus (reference)

> Verbatim KO corpus `013_etc/13_omx_etc.md` for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

# oh-my-codex (omx) 잔여 교차 관심사

## 개요

본 문서는 oh-my-codex(OMX) 코드베이스에서 앞선 차원 문서([[00_omx_overview]] 기준: CLI/setup, AGENTS.md 계약, MCP, 훅, `$`-워크플로우, 역할 프롬프트, Rust 런타임, `.omx/` 상태)에 충분히 다루지 못한 **잔여 교차 관심사(cross-cutting concerns)**를 소스 기반으로 분석한다. 분석 대상은 `/Users/jun/developer/codex/oh-my-codex`의 실제 소스다: TypeScript `src/`(40여 모듈), Rust `crates/`(6개), `prompts/`·`skills/`·`templates/`. 패키지 버전은 `0.18.6`(`package.json`).

> **OMX의 구조적 전제**: OMX는 자체 LLM 루프를 갖지 않는 **Codex CLI 컨트롤 플레인**이다. 따라서 [[13_cc_etc]](Claude Code)의 잔여 관심사가 "런타임 내부 구현"(프로바이더 SDK 래핑, 1P 이벤트 로깅 등)에 집중되는 반면, OMX의 잔여 관심사는 **Codex 외곽에서 세션을 띄우고·인증을 돌리고·알림을 쏘고·git 워크트리를 관리하는 오케스트레이션 레이어**에 집중된다. 이 비대칭 자체가 가장 큰 발견이다.

본 문서는 다음 항목을 다룬다: Git/VCS 통합, 모델 라우팅, 에러 처리/복구, 텔레메트리/관측성, 인증(멀티슬롯 쿼터 로테이션), 멀티모달(imagegen), 이벤트/알림, 피처 플래그, 패키징, 테스트, i18n. **omx에 실재하지 않는 항목은 솔직히 명시**한다.

---

## 1. Git/VCS 통합

OMX의 Git 통합은 [[13_cc_etc]]처럼 "프롬프트로 LLM에게 안전 규칙을 주입"하는 측면과, **Rust/TS 런타임이 직접 `git`을 spawn해 워크트리·리베이스를 조정**하는 측면 두 갈래로 나뉜다. 후자가 OMX 특유다.

### 1.1 git-master 프롬프트의 안전 계약

`prompts/git-master.md`(역할 프롬프트)는 git 전담 에이전트의 안전 규칙을 텍스트로 명시한다(`<scope_guard>`):
  • Work ALONE. Task tool and agent spawning are BLOCKED.
  • Detect commit style first: analyze last 30 commits ...
  • Never rebase main/master.
  • Use --force-with-lease, never --force.
  • Stash dirty files before rebasing.
  • Plan files (.omx/plans/*.md) are READ-ONLY.

[[13_cc_etc]]의 "Git Safety Protocol"(`NEVER push --force`, `NEVER reset --hard`)과 동형이지만, OMX는 이를 **단일 역할 프롬프트로 격리**한다. 또한 커밋 스타일을 `git log -30 --pretty=format:"%s"`로 먼저 탐지해 언어(영/한)·포맷(semantic/plain)을 맞추는 절차를 강제한다(`<explore>`).

### 1.2 팀 워크트리 격리 (src/team/worktree.ts)

`$team` 워커는 같은 레포에서 충돌 없이 병렬 작업하기 위해 **워커마다 git worktree를 분리**한다. `src/team/worktree.ts`는 `child_process`로 직접 git을 호출한다:

| 작업 | git 호출 | 라인 |
| --- | --- | :---: |
| 레포 루트 탐지 | `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` | 68 |
| 브랜치명 검증 | `git check-ref-format --branch <name>` | 105 |
| 브랜치 존재 확인 | `git show-ref --verify --quiet refs/heads/<name>` | 116 |
| dirty 판정 | `git status --porcelain [--untracked-files=all]` | 124,137 |
| 워크트리 정리 | `git worktree prune` | 193 |
| 공통 git dir | `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` | 259 |
| 워크트리 추가 | `git worktree add ...` | 456 |
| 워크트리 제거 | `git worktree remove --force <path>` | 510,548 |
| 브랜치 삭제 | `git branch -D <branch>` | 529 |

`WorktreeMode`는 `{enabled:false}` / `{enabled,detached,name}` 합으로 모델링되며, scope는 `'launch' | 'team' | 'autoresearch'` 세 가지다. 즉 단일 세션·팀·연구 모드마다 워크트리 전략이 다르다.

### 1.3 팀 운영 커밋 위생 (src/team/commit-hygiene.ts)

`$team`은 단순 fan-out이 아니라 **리더가 워커 브랜치를 통합하는 git 상태 머신**이다. `commit-hygiene.ts`는 운영 커밋을 8종으로 타입화하고(`TeamOperationalCommitKind`), 각 결과를 append-only 로그(`TeamOperationalCommitEntry`)로 기록한다:

TEAM_OPERATIONAL_COMMIT_KINDS: auto_checkpoint, integration_merge, integration_cherry_pick, cross_rebase, worker_clean_rebase, leader_integration_attempt, shutdown_checkpoint, shutdown_merge 상태: applied | noop | conflict | skipped


`cross_rebase`/`integration_merge`/`cherry_pick`의 분류는 워커 산출물을 리더 HEAD에 통합하는 과정을 추적 가능하게 만든다. `package.json`에는 `test:team:cross-rebase-smoke` 회귀 스위트가 별도로 존재한다.

### 1.4 commit Lore 가드 (src/config/commit-lore-guard.ts)

`OMX_LORE_COMMIT_GUARD` 환경변수(또는 `config.toml`의 `shell_environment_policy.set` / `env`)로 켜지는 커밋 가드다. PostToolUse 훅(`src/scripts/codex-native-pre-post.ts`)에서 참조되어, "lore"(서사/문서) 커밋에 대한 정책을 적용한다. doctor에서도 활성 여부를 점검한다(`src/cli/doctor.ts`).

> **평가:** Git/VCS는 OMX에서 가장 성숙한 잔여 관심사다. CC가 "프롬프트 안전 규칙 + worktree 감지"에 머무는 반면, OMX는 **멀티워커 통합을 git worktree + 8종 운영 커밋 로그로 직접 오케스트레이션**한다.

---

## 2. 모델 라우팅 / 프로바이더 추상화

OMX는 LLM을 직접 호출하지 않으므로 [[13_cc_etc]]식 "프로바이더 SDK 래핑(Bedrock/Vertex)"이 **없다**. 대신 **Codex에 어떤 모델을 넘길지 결정하는 라우팅 레이어**가 모델 라우팅의 본체다(`src/config/models.ts`).

### 2.1 3-레인 모델 분류와 해석 순서

`models.ts`는 모델을 3개 레인으로 나눠 기본값을 하드코딩한다:

| 레인 | 기본값 상수 | env override |
| --- | --- | --- |
| frontier | `DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL = 'gpt-5.5'` | `OMX_DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL` |
| standard | `DEFAULT_STANDARD_MODEL = 'gpt-5.4-mini'` | `OMX_DEFAULT_STANDARD_MODEL` |
| spark(저복잡도) | `DEFAULT_SPARK_MODEL = 'gpt-5.3-codex-spark'` | `OMX_DEFAULT_SPARK_MODEL` / `OMX_SPARK_MODEL` |
| team child | `DEFAULT_TEAM_CHILD_MODEL = standard` | `OMX_TEAM_CHILD_MODEL` |

해석 순서는 레인마다 정의된다. 예: `getMainDefaultModel()`은 `OMX_DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL` → `config.toml`의 `model` → `DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL`. `getModelForMode(mode)`는 `.omx-config.json`의 `models[mode]` → `models.default` → main default. standard 레인은 명시 override가 없으면 **frontier를 상속**해 리더-워커 모델 동기화를 유지한다(`models.ts:268`).

### 2.2 에이전트별 reasoning effort 라우팅

`models.ts`는 모델 강제보다 **`reasoning_effort` 조정을 선호**한다([[00_omx_overview]] §6.2와 정합). `.omx-config.json`의 `agentReasoning` 블록이 역할별 effort를 `'low'|'medium'|'high'|'xhigh'`로 매핑한다(`getAgentReasoningOverride`). 역할명은 `/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]*$/`로 정규화·검증된다.

### 2.3 Codex 프로바이더 env 패스스루

`readActiveProviderEnvOverrides()`(`models.ts:179`)는 Codex `config.toml`의 `model_providers.<active>.env_key`를 읽어, 해당 env 변수만 자식 프로세스에 전달한다. 즉 OMX는 프로바이더 인증 자체를 다루지 않고 **Codex가 선언한 env_key를 중계**만 한다 — CC의 `getProvider()`식 직접 분기와 대조적이다.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Req["모델 요청 (mode/agent)"] --> ModeChk{".omx-config.json<br/>models[mode]?"}
    ModeChk -->|yes| Use1["모드별 모델"]
    ModeChk -->|no| DefKey{"models.default?"}
    DefKey -->|yes| Use2["default 모델"]
    DefKey -->|no| EnvChk{"OMX_DEFAULT_*<br/>env?"}
    EnvChk -->|yes| Use3["env 모델"]
    EnvChk -->|no| CfgChk{"config.toml<br/>model?"}
    CfgChk -->|yes| Use4["Codex config 모델"]
    CfgChk -->|no| HC["하드코딩 기본값<br/>(gpt-5.5 등)"]
    Use1 & Use2 & Use3 & Use4 & HC --> Effort["agentReasoning<br/>effort override"]
    Effort --> Codex["codex 자식 프로세스<br/>(--model / -c)"]

3. 에러 처리 / 복구 패턴

OMX의 에러 처리는 중앙 errors/ 모듈이 없고(CC와 대조), 관심사별로 분산된 "복구 권고(advisory)" 패턴이 특징이다.

3.1 PostToolUse 에러 분류와 advisory (codex-native-pre-post.ts)

src/scripts/codex-native-pre-post.ts는 도구 결과를 분석해 재시도 대신 무엇을 할지 권고를 주입한다. 3가지 신호를 구분한다(라인 1235~1274):

신호 트리거 권고
MCP transport 실패 (McpTransportFailureSignal) MCP 도구가 transport/server 연결 상실 "맹목 재시도 말고 OMX CLI 패리티 표면으로 폴백, 상태 보존"
Bash setup 실패 command not found / permission denied / 파일 누락 "PATH·의존성·권한·경로를 검증 후 재시도"
비영(非零) 종료지만 유용한 출력 non-zero exit + informative stdout "출력을 검토·보고 후 재시도(단순 실패로 단정 금지)"

핵심 설계: OMX는 자동 재시도 루프를 돌리기보다, 모델에게 구조화된 복구 지침을 텍스트로 돌려준다. MCP가 죽으면 동일 도구의 CLI/JSON 패리티([[00_omx_overview]] §3.1)로 우회하라는 것이 단일 실패점 회피의 핵심이다.

3.2 쿼터 에러 탐지 → 인증 로테이션 (auth/quota-detector.ts)

가장 정교한 자동 복구는 인증 레이어에 있다(§7 상세). isQuotaError()는 Codex stderr/stdout/structuredError를 합쳐 패턴 매칭한다:

const DEFAULT_PATTERNS = [/\b429\b/i, /\bquota\b/i, /rate\s*limit(?:ed|s)?/i, /too\s+many\s+requests/i];

사용자 정의 quotaPatterns도 정규식으로 추가 가능하며, 정규식 컴파일 실패 시 substring 매칭으로 폴백한다(quota-detector.ts:39-45). 쿼터 감지 시 §7의 슬롯 로테이션이 발동한다.

3.3 알림 HTTP 클라이언트의 타임아웃/프록시 (notifications/http-client.ts)

알림 전송은 독립 HTTP 클라이언트를 쓴다(src/notifications/http-client.ts). globalThis.fetch가 있으면 AbortSignal.timeout(timeoutMs)로, 없으면 native http/https request로 폴백한다(라인 155~165). 프록시 env(HTTPS_PROXY/http_proxy/all_proxy)를 존중하고, HTTP/HTTPS 프로토콜만 허용한다. CC의 OTLP 재시도 계층 같은 지수 백오프 재시도는 없고, 대신 쿨다운/디듀프로 폭주를 막는다(§5.3).

3.4 spawn 에러 분류 (auth/hotswap.ts)

runCodexDirect()는 classifySpawnError()로 codex 실행 실패를 missing(PATH에 없음)/blocked(권한 차단)로 분류해 사용자 친화 메시지를 만든다(hotswap.ts:84-92).

평가: 중앙 에러 모듈은 없지만, "재시도 대신 복구 권고 + 인증 로테이션 + 알림 쿨다운"이라는 OMX식 분산 복구는 컨트롤 플레인 성격에 적합하다.

4. 텔레메트리 / 관측성

OMX는 외부 텔레메트리 백엔드로 데이터를 보내지 않는다. tengu_ 같은 1P 이벤트 로깅, OpenTelemetry/OTLP, GrowthBook/Statsig 같은 원격 수집이 소스에 존재하지 않는다. 대신 관측성은 전부 로컬 파일 + 로컬 HUD/Trace로 구현된다 — 프라이버시·자율성 우선의 설계 선택이다.

4.1 Trace MCP — 로컬 JSONL 기반 (mcp/trace-server.ts)

omx_trace 서버(src/mcp/trace-server.ts, omx-trace v0.1.0)는 .omx/logs/의 turns-*.jsonl(notify 훅이 생성)을 읽어 타임라인/통계를 제공한다:

trace_timeline   ← 턴별 input/output preview 시간순 정렬
trace_summary    ← 집계 통계
TraceEntry: { timestamp, type, thread_id?, turn_id?, input_preview?, output_preview? }

keepLastEntries()는 힙 유사 구조로 최신 N개만 메모리에 유지해 대용량 로그를 견딘다(trace-server.ts:36). 이것이 OMX의 관측성 데이터 모델이다 — 원격 전송 없이 로컬 JSONL을 질의.

4.2 HUD — 로컬 상태 렌더링

omx hud([[00_omx_overview]] §7.4, src/hud/)는 .omx/ 상태를 터미널에 렌더링한다. --watch(1s 폴링)/--json/--tmux/--preset 플래그로 관측 수준을 조절한다. 이는 원격 대시보드가 아닌 로컬·실시간 관측이다.

4.3 비교: CC와의 비대칭

관측 차원 Claude Code oh-my-codex
1P 이벤트 로깅 tengu_* Anthropic 수집 없음
3P OpenTelemetry OTLP grpc/http 지원 없음
메트릭 카운터 claude_code.* 없음
로컬 트레이스 (제한적) omx_trace JSONL + HUD
이벤트 알림 (Slack 체크 프롬프트) OpenClaw/Discord/Telegram/Slack 푸시(§5)

즉 OMX의 "텔레메트리"는 **관측(local trace/HUD) + 알림(push)**으로 갈라지며, 분석용 원격 수집은 의도적으로 부재한다.

5. 이벤트 / 알림 시스템

OMX의 가장 풍부한 잔여 관심사 중 하나다. CC가 알림을 프롬프트 수준 "Slack 체크"로만 다루는 반면, OMX는 다채널 알림 + OpenClaw 게이트웨이라는 본격 이벤트 시스템을 갖는다(src/notifications/, src/openclaw/).

5.1 다채널 알림 (notifications/types.ts)

NotificationChannel은 5종을 지원한다(types.ts:29-33):

discord | discord-bot | telegram | slack | webhook

각 채널은 전용 config(Discord webhookUrl, Discord Bot token+channelId, Telegram, Slack incoming webhook, generic webhook)를 가지며, custom_webhook_command는 OpenClaw 게이트웨이로 정규화된다(types.ts:150). 전송은 §3.3의 프록시-인식 HTTP 클라이언트를 쓴다.

5.2 OpenClaw 게이트웨이 — 훅 이벤트를 외부로 (openclaw/)

src/openclaw/는 Codex 훅 이벤트를 외부 게이트웨이에 깨우는(wake) 브리지다. 이벤트 enum(openclaw/types.ts:13):

session-start | session-end | session-idle | ask-user-question | stop

각 이벤트는 게이트웨이(HTTP 또는 CLI command)와 instruction에 매핑된다(OpenClawHookMapping). HTTP 게이트웨이는 HTTPS 필수(localhost만 HTTP 허용), 기본 타임아웃 10s. command 게이트웨이는 {{variable}} 템플릿을 셸 이스케이프 후 보간하며 타임아웃 정밀도는 gateway timeout > OMX_OPENCLAW_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS > 5000ms로 클램프된다(types.ts). 이는 "에이전트가 질문을 던지거나 멈췄을 때 사람에게 푸시"하는 휴먼-인-더-루프 채널이다.

5.3 쿨다운 / 디듀프 / 아이들 (이벤트 throttling)

알림 폭주 방지가 정교하다:

  • dispatch-cooldown.ts: 최소 디스패치 간격. OMX_DISPATCH_COOLDOWN_SECONDS(기본 60s) > config, 상태 파일 .omx/state/[sessions/<id>/]dispatch-notif-cooldown.json. 0이면 비활성.
  • idle-cooldown.ts: 아이들 알림 전용 쿨다운.
  • lifecycle-dedupe.ts: 라이프사이클 이벤트 중복 제거.

이 throttling 레이어가 CC의 "이벤트 샘플링(shouldSampleEvent)"에 대응하는 OMX식 메커니즘이다 — 다만 목적이 분석 데이터 절감이 아니라 사람에게 가는 푸시 절감이다.

5.4 reply-listener — 양방향 알림

notifications/reply-listener.ts(34KB, 최대 파일)는 알림에 대한 답신을 수신하는 리스너다. test:reply-listener:live 스크립트로 라이브 테스트된다. 즉 OMX 알림은 단방향 통지가 아니라 사람의 응답을 다시 세션으로 주입하는 양방향 채널이다.

6. 멀티모달 (imagegen / visual)

CC의 멀티모달이 "이미지 첨부 입력 + 스크린샷 출력(sharp/resvg)"인 반면, OMX의 멀티모달은 Codex 내장 image_gen을 턴 경계 너머로 이어붙이는 continuation과 시각 검증 게이트다.

6.1 imagegen continuation (src/imagegen/continuation.ts)

Codex 내장 image_gen 호출이 한 턴 안에서 완결되지 못할 때, prepareImagegenContinuation()은 Stop 훅 팔로우업을 큐잉한다:

.omx/state/sessions/<id>/imagegen-pending.json  ← ImagegenContinuationRecord{version,id,artifact_name,after,status:"pending",resume_instruction}

resume_instruction은 "생성된 최신 이미지를 $CODEX_HOME/generated_images/<session>에서 찾아 워크스페이스로 복사 → crop/post-process → visual QA/visual-verdict 게이트 → Ralph 진행 상태 갱신 후 계속"하라는 구체 지침이다(continuation.ts:81-93). injectExecFollowup()으로 다음 Stop 체크포인트에 전달된다. 즉 OMX 멀티모달은 "이미지 생성 완료가 곧 작업 종료"를 막는 지속성 패턴이다.

6.2 visual-verdict 게이트 (src/visual/)

src/visual/verdict.ts + constants.ts는 $visual-ralph/$visual-verdict 워크플로우의 시각 검증 판정을 다룬다. ralph-progress.json에 visual-verdict JSON이 기록되어([[00_omx_overview]] §8.1) 시각 산출물도 검증-게이트의 대상이 된다.

부재 명시: OMX에는 CC의 sharp/resvg 같은 자체 이미지 렌더링·스크린샷·클립보드 복사가 없다. 이미지 생성은 Codex 내장 image_gen에 위임하고, OMX는 continuation·검증만 소유한다.

7. 인증 / API 키 관리 — 멀티슬롯 쿼터 로테이션

OMX 인증의 핵심은 CC식 "토큰 소스 우선순위·OAuth 플로우"가 아니라, 여러 Codex 계정(auth.json)을 슬롯으로 보관하고 쿼터 소진 시 자동 전환하는 hotswap이다(src/auth/). 이는 OMX 특유의 강력한 자산이다.

7.1 슬롯 저장소 (auth/storage.ts)

각 계정의 auth.json을 named slot으로 보관한다. AuthMetadata는 슬롯별 lastUsedAt/lastQuotaAt/exhaustedAt을 추적한다. 보안이 견고하다:

  • 원자적 쓰기: atomicWriteFile()은 *.tmp-<pid>-<ts>-<rand> 임시파일 → rename (storage.ts:36).
  • 권한 고정: AUTH_FILE_MODE로 chmod (0600류), ensurePrivateDir.
  • 심볼릭 링크 차단: assertNoSymlink() — auth 타겟이 심링크면 거부(공격 벡터 차단).
  • 슬롯명 검증: validateSlotName().

7.2 로테이션 정책 (auth/rotation.ts, config.ts)

AuthRotationMode = "round-robin" | "priority" | "manual"(기본 round-robin). config는 .omx/config.toml의 [omx.auth] 테이블에서 cwd → omx.toml → ~/.omx로 병합된다(config.ts:48). buildRotationPlan()이 모드별 슬롯 순서를 만들고, nextSlotAfter()가 소진 슬롯을 건너뛰며 다음을 고른다.

7.3 hotswap 루프 (auth/hotswap.ts)

omx auth --hotswap의 핵심 루프:

sequenceDiagram
    participant H as runAuthHotswap
    participant C as codex (child)
    participant S as slot storage
    H->>S: useSlot(slot0) → live auth.json
    H->>C: spawn codex (slot0)
    C-->>H: exit status
    alt status == 0
        H-->>H: return 0 (성공)
    else isQuotaError(stderr/429)
        H->>S: markSlotQuota(slot0)
        H->>S: findLatestRolloutSession() (resume 대상)
        H->>S: nextSlotAfter() → slot1
        H->>C: codex resume <sessionId> (preserved flags, slot1)
    else 기타 에러
        H-->>H: return status (비-쿼터는 즉시 종료)
    end
  • 쿼터 감지 시 markSlotQuota로 표시하고, findLatestRolloutSession()으로 중단된 Codex 세션을 찾아 codex resume <id>로 이어붙인다(hotswap.ts:208). 즉 계정 전환이 작업 손실 없이 일어난다.
  • buildResumeArgsWithPreservedFlags()는 -c/--config/--model/-m/--dangerously-bypass-...를 보존해 재개한다(hotswap.ts:100).
  • mode === "manual"이면 자동 전환 없이 사용자에게 omx auth use <slot>을 안내한다.

7.4 시크릿 redaction (auth/redact.ts)

모든 codex stderr와 에러 메시지는 모델/로그에 닿기 전 redactAuthSecrets()를 거친다. 5종 패턴을 마스킹한다:

JSON_OAUTH_TOKEN_FIELD ("access_token"/"refresh_token"/"id_token" 값)
access/refresh/id_token 라인, bearer <...>, sk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,}, session/auth/api_token

CC의 25종 시크릿 패턴보다 좁지만(OMX는 OAuth 토큰·sk-키 중심), 모든 codex stderr 스트림에 일괄 적용된다는 점이 다르다(hotswap.ts:80,215,219).

평가: 멀티슬롯 쿼터 로테이션 + 세션 resume은 CC에 없는 OMX 고유 기능이다. "쿼터 소진 → 다음 계정 → 같은 세션 재개"는 장시간 자율 실행에 직접적 가치가 있다.

8. 피처 플래그

OMX에는 CC의 GrowthBook/Statsig 같은 원격 피처 플래그 시스템이 없다. 대신 두 종류의 로컬 플래그가 있다.

8.1 Codex 호환 피처 플래그 탐지 (config/codex-feature-flags.ts)

이것은 "OMX 기능 롤아웃"이 아니라 설치된 Codex CLI가 어떤 피처를 노출하는지 탐지하는 호환성 플래그다. [features].hooks(현행) vs [features].codex_hooks(레거시) 철자를 판별한다:

resolveCodexHookFeatureFlag():
  featureNames.has("hooks") → "hooks"
  featureNames.has("codex_hooks") → "codex_hooks"
  codexVersion >= 0.130.0 → "hooks"
  else fallback

parseCodexCliVersion() + isCodexCliVersionAtLeast([0,130,0])로 버전 기반 폴백을, supportsCodexPluginScopedHooks()로 plugin_hooks 지원을 점검한다. 즉 OMX는 Codex 버전·피처에 적응하는 플래그를 갖되, 자체 A/B 롤아웃 플래그는 없다.

8.2 환경변수 기반 기능 게이트

나머지 기능 토글은 단순 env 변수다: OMX_LORE_COMMIT_GUARD(§1.4), OMX_DISPATCH_COOLDOWN_SECONDS(§5.3), OMX_DEFAULT_*_MODEL(§2), OMX_OPENCLAW_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS(§5.2), OMX_ROOT. 원격 제어가 아닌 로컬 결정론적 설정이다.

9. 패키징 / 배포

OMX는 하이브리드 패키징(npm + Rust cargo-dist)이라는 점에서 CC(순수 npm 번들)와 다르다.

9.1 npm 패키지 (package.json)

"name": "oh-my-codex", "version": "0.18.6",
"bin": { "omx": "dist/cli/omx.js" }, "type": "module",
"engines": { "node": ">=20" },
"dependencies": { "@iarna/toml", "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk", "zod" }
  • CC와 대조: 실제 런타임 의존성을 가진다(toml 파서, MCP SDK, zod). CC는 dependencies: {} 제로 번들이었다.
  • files에 dist/, crates/, skills/, prompts/, templates/, plugins/, src/scripts/를 포함 — TS 산출물 + Rust 소스 + 에셋을 함께 배포.
  • postinstall: postinstall-bootstrap.js — dist/scripts/postinstall.js를 동적 import하되, 없거나 실패하면 비치명적으로 스킵(postinstall-bootstrap.js:16). 설치 견고성 우선.

9.2 Rust 크로스 컴파일 (dist-workspace.toml, Cargo.toml)

crates/의 6개 워크스페이스(모두 v0.18.6)는 cargo-dist 0.31.0으로 빌드된다. dist-workspace.toml의 타겟:

aarch64-apple-darwin, aarch64-unknown-linux-{gnu,musl},
x86_64-apple-darwin, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, x86_64-unknown-linux-{gnu,musl}

다만 installers = []이고 *"custom release.yml을 유지"*한다고 명시 — cargo-dist의 자동 워크플로우 대신 수동 릴리스 파이프라인을 쓴다. RELEASE_PROTOCOL.md가 별도 존재.

9.3 빌드 파이프라인 (package.json scripts)

build:full = build(tsc) + build:explore:release + build:sparkshell + build:api. prepack은 verify:native-agents + sync:plugin + verify:plugin-bundle + native 에셋 정리를 강제한다. 플러그인 미러(plugins/oh-my-codex)는 sync-plugin-mirror.js --check로 SSOT 동기화가 검증된다.

10. 테스트 인프라

CC와의 최대 차이점. CC는 디컴파일 번들이라 테스트가 보이지 않았으나(N/A), OMX는 오픈소스로 방대한 테스트 인프라를 노출한다.

10.1 규모

  • 346개 *.test.ts 파일, 36개 __tests__/ 디렉토리.
  • 러너: node --test(Node 내장) + c8 커버리지. src/scripts/run-test-files.js가 dist/를 재귀 실행.
  • Rust: cargo test(예: cargo test -p omx-explore-harness).

10.2 커버리지 게이트 (package.json, COVERAGE.md)

coverage:team-critical은 dist/team·dist/state에 lines≥78 / functions≥90 / branches≥70 / statements≥78을 강제한다(--check-coverage). 즉 팀 런타임은 함수 90% 커버리지가 CI 게이트다.

COVERAGE.md는 oh-my-claudecode(OMC) 대비 >=90% 패리티를 목표로 명시한 기능 매트릭스를 유지한다(Last Updated 2026-02-22, TOTAL ~95%). 예: Agent Definitions 100%, Code Intelligence ~58%(pragmatic 7/12 tools), Python REPL 0%.

10.3 회귀 스위트 명명

특정 버그/PR에 묶인 회귀 테스트가 스크립트로 격리된다: test:recent-bug-regressions, test:team:cross-rebase-smoke, test:ralph-persistence, test:explicit-terminal-contract, test:compat:rust(OMX_COMPAT_TARGET로 Rust 바이너리와 TS 동작 동등성 검증). 이는 "버그마다 회귀 테스트를 남긴다"는 규율의 증거다.

10.4 스모크/라이브 테스트

smoke:packed-install(packed npm 설치 시뮬레이션), test:sparkshell, test:reply-listener:live(실제 알림 답신 라이브 테스트), demo-team-e2e.sh.

11. 국제화 / 접근성

11.1 i18n — 부재

국제화 프레임워크는 없다. grep으로 확인한 i18n/gettext/Intl./toLocaleString/localize/translate 매칭은 전부 무관하다 — 유일한 translate* 함수는 src/team/tmux-session.ts의 translatePathForMsys()(Windows MSYS 경로 변환)와 translateWorkerLaunchArgsForCli()(워커 CLI 인자 변환)로, 언어 번역이 아니라 경로/인자 변환이다. 모든 사용자 대면 문자열은 영어 하드코딩이다.

단, git-master 프롬프트는 커밋 메시지 언어를 탐지해 맞춘다(영/한, §1.1) — 산출물 다국어화는 LLM에 위임하는 셈이다.

11.2 접근성 / 크로스플랫폼

  • Windows 부분 지원: translatePathForMsys, windowsHide(hotswap.ts:76), chmod의 process.platform !== "win32" 가드(storage.ts:47). 단 §9.2의 Rust 타겟에 windows-msvc가 있으나, omx-explore는 WINDOWS_UNSUPPORTED_ALLOWLIST_MESSAGE로 Windows 미지원([[00_omx_overview]] §7.3). tmux 의존 워크플로우도 POSIX 전제.
  • 출력 redaction: 시크릿 마스킹(§7.4)은 보안 접근성 요소.

12. 기술 참조

주요 파일 경로

영역 파일

팀 커밋 위생 /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/team/commit-hygiene.ts

슬롯 저장소(원자/심링크 방어) /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/auth/storage.ts
로테이션 정책 /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/auth/rotation.ts

알림 HTTP 클라이언트 /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/notifications/http-client.ts

imagegen continuation /Users/jun/developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/imagegen/continuation.ts

환경변수 참조

변수 용도
OMX_DEFAULT_FRONTIER_MODEL / _STANDARD_ / _SPARK_ 레인별 기본 모델
OMX_TEAM_CHILD_MODEL 팀 워커 모델
OMX_LORE_COMMIT_GUARD lore 커밋 가드 활성
OMX_DISPATCH_COOLDOWN_SECONDS 알림 디스패치 쿨다운(기본 60s)
OMX_OPENCLAW_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS OpenClaw command 게이트웨이 타임아웃
OMX_ROOT .omx/ 루트 override
CODEX_HOME / CODEX_SQLITE_HOME Codex 홈(hotswap이 주입)
HTTPS_PROXY / http_proxy / all_proxy 알림 HTTP 프록시

성숙도 평가

영역 성숙도 비고
Git/VCS(worktree·커밋 위생) ★★★★★ 멀티워커 git 오케스트레이션
모델 라우팅(3-레인) ★★★★☆ env/config 해석 체인, effort override
에러 처리(분산 advisory) ★★★☆☆ 중앙 모듈 없음, 복구 권고 중심
텔레메트리(원격 수집) ★☆☆☆☆ 원격 부재 — 의도적
관측성(로컬 trace/HUD) ★★★★☆ JSONL trace + 실시간 HUD
이벤트/알림 ★★★★★ 5채널 + OpenClaw + 양방향 reply
인증(멀티슬롯 로테이션) ★★★★★ 쿼터 자동 전환 + 세션 resume
멀티모달(imagegen) ★★★☆☆ continuation·검증만, 렌더링 위임
피처 플래그 ★★☆☆☆ Codex 호환 탐지 + env, 원격 롤아웃 없음
패키징(npm+cargo-dist) ★★★★☆ 하이브리드, 크로스 컴파일
테스트 인프라 ★★★★★ 346 테스트 + 커버리지 게이트
i18n ★☆☆☆☆ 부재(영어 하드코딩)

cli-jaw 비교 시사점

  • (멀티슬롯 인증 로테이션 이식 가치) OMX src/auth/의 "쿼터 감지(isQuotaError) → 슬롯 전환 → 중단 세션 resume"(hotswap.ts:183-209)은 장시간 자율 실행에서 단일 계정 쿼터 한계를 우회하는 검증된 패턴이다. [[cli-jaw]]가 동일 한계를 겪는다면, 원자적 쓰기·심링크 차단·0600 권한(storage.ts)까지 포함한 이 슬롯 모델이 직접 참고 대상이다 — 단, 시크릿 redaction(redact.ts)을 모든 자식 stderr 스트림에 일괄 적용하는 규율을 함께 가져가야 한다.

  • (텔레메트리 부재는 설계 선택) OMX는 원격 텔레메트리(1P 이벤트/OTLP/GrowthBook)를 전혀 두지 않고 관측을 로컬 trace JSONL + HUD로 한정했다. cli-jaw가 프라이버시·자율성을 우선한다면, "원격 수집 0 + 로컬 질의 가능한 JSONL"이라는 OMX 모델이 CC식 이중 텔레메트리보다 운영·신뢰 부담이 적다. 단 분석·집계가 필요해지면 로컬 trace를 옵트인 익스포트하는 경로를 추가로 설계해야 한다.

  • (알림을 1급 이벤트 채널로) OMX는 알림을 단순 통지가 아니라 휴먼-인-더-루프 양방향 채널로 본다(5채널 + OpenClaw ask-user-question/stop 이벤트 + reply-listener 응답 주입 + 쿨다운/디듀프). cli-jaw가 사람 개입이 필요한 자율 실행을 노린다면, push 채널 + 응답 수신 + throttling(쿨다운/디듀프/아이들)을 한 세트로 설계하는 것이 폭주 없이 협업을 가능케 한다.

  • (테스트·커버리지를 계약으로) OMX는 346 테스트와 coverage:team-critical(functions≥90%) CI 게이트, 버그별 명명된 회귀 스위트(test:recent-bug-regressions/cross-rebase-smoke), TS↔Rust 동등성 검증(test:compat:rust)을 갖춘다. cli-jaw가 멀티런타임(예: Node+Rust) 구조라면, 동작 동등성을 OMX_COMPAT_TARGET식 크로스-구현 테스트로 못박는 패턴이 회귀 방지에 직접적이다.

변경 기록

  • 2026-05-29: oh-my-codex v0.18.6 소스 기반 최초 작성. Git/VCS(team/worktree.ts·commit-hygiene.ts·git-master.md), 3-레인 모델 라우팅(config/models.ts), 분산 에러 advisory(codex-native-pre-post.ts·quota-detector.ts), 로컬 관측성(trace-server.ts·HUD, 원격 텔레메트리 부재 명시), 5채널 알림+OpenClaw 양방향 이벤트(notifications/·openclaw/), 멀티슬롯 쿼터 로테이션 인증(auth/hotswap.ts·storage.ts·rotation.ts·redact.ts), imagegen continuation 멀티모달(imagegen/continuation.ts·visual/), Codex 호환 피처 플래그(codex-feature-flags.ts), npm+cargo-dist 하이브리드 패키징, 346 테스트+커버리지 게이트, i18n 부재 확인 포함. CC 대비 비대칭(원격 텔레메트리 부재 vs 멀티슬롯 인증·다채널 알림 풍부) 분석.
30

Axis 14: Web search

AXIS

014_web-search/14_omx_web-search.md

Overview

Axis 14: Web search — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 171_oh-my-codex/ — no first-party web-search MCP; internet research delegates to Codex web_search Responses tool + agent prompts; local knowledge via omx_wiki / omx_code_intel.

  • OMX is a Codex workflow/plugin layer. Its six first-party MCP servers cover state, memory, code intelligence, trace, wiki, and Hermes coordination — none implement HTTP crawl or search indexing. Web research is a host concern (OpenA
  • flowchart TD
  • OMX["OMX plugin / config generator"] --> Gen["mergeConfig → config.toml"]
  • Gen --> WS["web_search = true (user/feature)"]
  • WS --> Codex["121_openai-codex codex-rs"]
  • OMX --> FP["First-party MCPs"]
  • FP --> Wiki["omx_wiki — local KB"]
  • FP --> CI["omx_code_intel — LSP/AST"]
  • OMX --> Prompt["researcher.md agent"]
  • Prompt --> Agent["Use web search/fetch + Context7"]

Topics covered

  • Architecture
    1. First-party MCP inventory (no web search)
  • Web-adjacent first-party surfaces
    1. Codex host web_search delegation
  • Config generator preserves user flag
  • What web_search = true activates
    1. Researcher agent prompt (methodology layer)
  • Repo research surface (:40-48)
    1. OMX vs OMO web search split
    1. Historical note: $research removal
    1. omx_wiki vs internet search
    1. Code intelligence as search substitute
    1. Hermes coordination MCP
    1. Setup path for full web research stack

  Gen --> WS["web_search = true (user/feature)"]

  OMX --> FP["First-party MCPs"]

// 171_oh-my-codex/src/config/omx-first-party-mcp.ts:15-58
const OMX_FIRST_PARTY_MCP_SPECS = [
  { name: "omx_state", entrypoint: "state-server.js", /* session state */ },
  { name: "omx_memory", entrypoint: "memory-server.js", /* project memory */ },
  { name: "omx_code_intel", entrypoint: "code-intel-server.js", /* LSP + AST */ },
  { name: "omx_trace", entrypoint: "trace-server.js", /* agent timeline */ },
  { name: "omx_wiki", entrypoint: "wiki-server.js", /* persistent project KB */ },
  { name: "omx_hermes", entrypoint: "hermes-server.js", /* dispatch coordination */ },
] as const;

  'model = "o3"',
  '[features]',
  'web_search = true',
].join('\n');
await mergeConfig(configPath, wd);

README / docs

// :73-76 (tools section)
Use web search/fetch for official docs, versioned references, release notes...
Use Context7 MCP for resolved library IDs when registered; fall back to web search otherwise.

README / docs

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/protocol/src/models.rs (lineage reference)
pub enum WebSearchAction {
    Search { query, queries },
    OpenPage { url },
    FindInPage { url, pattern },
    Other,
}

README / docs

// omx-first-party-mcp.ts:90-100
command: process.execPath,
args: [join(pkgRoot, "dist", "mcp", spec.entrypoint)],
startupTimeoutSec: spec.startupTimeoutSec, // 5-10s per server

README / docs

rg -n web_search /Users/jun/Developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/
wc -c /Users/jun/Developer/codex/014_web-search/14_omx_web-search.md  # T2 floor 12288

Main analysis

Oh My Codex — Web Search Implementation

Analysis snapshot: 171_oh-my-codex/ — no first-party web-search MCP; internet research delegates to Codex web_search Responses tool + agent prompts; local knowledge via omx_wiki / omx_code_intel.

OMX is a Codex workflow/plugin layer. Its six first-party MCP servers cover state, memory, code intelligence, trace, wiki, and Hermes coordination — none implement HTTP crawl or search indexing. Web research is a host concern (OpenAI Codex ToolSpec::WebSearch) plus optional user MCPs configured outside OMX.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  OMX["OMX plugin / config generator"] --> Gen["mergeConfig → config.toml"]
  Gen --> WS["web_search = true (user/feature)"]
  WS --> Codex["121_openai-codex codex-rs"]
  OMX --> FP["First-party MCPs"]
  FP --> Wiki["omx_wiki — local KB"]
  FP --> CI["omx_code_intel — LSP/AST"]
  OMX --> Prompt["researcher.md agent"]
  Prompt --> Agent["Use web search/fetch + Context7"]

1. First-party MCP inventory (no web search)

// 171_oh-my-codex/src/config/omx-first-party-mcp.ts:15-58
const OMX_FIRST_PARTY_MCP_SPECS = [
  { name: "omx_state", entrypoint: "state-server.js", /* session state */ },
  { name: "omx_memory", entrypoint: "memory-server.js", /* project memory */ },
  { name: "omx_code_intel", entrypoint: "code-intel-server.js", /* LSP + AST */ },
  { name: "omx_trace", entrypoint: "trace-server.js", /* agent timeline */ },
  { name: "omx_wiki", entrypoint: "wiki-server.js", /* persistent project KB */ },
  { name: "omx_hermes", entrypoint: "hermes-server.js", /* dispatch coordination */ },
] as const;

buildOmxPluginMcpManifest() exposes these via omx mcp-serve <target> (:103-127) — no websearch target exists.

Web-adjacent first-party surfaces

MCP Web role Lines
omx_wiki Local project knowledge — not internet omx-first-party-mcp.ts:45-49
omx_code_intel Repo-local AST/LSP search — not GitHub-wide :31-35
omx_hermes Dispatches subagents — may run researchers :52-56

2. Codex host web_search delegation

Config generator preserves user flag

// src/config/__tests__/generator-notify.test.ts:237-259
const existing = [
  'model = "o3"',
  '[features]',
  'web_search = true',
].join('\n');
await mergeConfig(configPath, wd);
// assert: web_search = true still present after OMX merge

OMX mergeConfig adds OMX keys (notify, multi_agent, goals) without clobbering existing web_search feature flag.

What web_search = true activates

See 014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md:

  • Rust ToolSpec::WebSearch in codex-rs/tools/src/tool_spec.rs
  • Modes live / cached / disabled via ~/.codex/config.toml
  • OpenAI Responses server pipeline (Bing + OAI-SearchBot + rerank)

OMX does not ship Rust sources — it only ensures Codex config allows the host tool.

3. Researcher agent prompt (methodology layer)

171_oh-my-codex/prompts/researcher.md defines external research behavior:

// :73-76 (tools section)
Use web search/fetch for official docs, versioned references, release notes...
Use Context7 MCP for resolved library IDs when registered; fall back to web search otherwise.

Repo research surface (:40-48)

  • gh search code, gh search repos for OSS discovery
  • Pinned SHA raw URLs: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<org>/<repo>/<sha>/<path>
  • Context7 when omx setup registered MCP
  • Citation format: org/repo@sha:path:Lx-Ly

This is prompt contract only — execution requires Codex web_search enabled and/or MCP servers configured by user/omx setup.

4. OMX vs OMO web search split

Layer OMX (171_oh-my-codex) OMO Ultimate (172_oh-my-openagent)
Built-in Exa MCP No createWebsearchConfig
Codex web_search Primary internet path When host is Codex
GitHub code search omx_code_intel (local) grep_app remote MCP
Docs researcher + Context7 user MCP builtin context7

5. Historical note: $research removal

CHANGELOG.md (~line 1779 in corpus) documents $research skill removal in favor of $external-context for web searches — OMX moved research orchestration from a single skill token to explicit agent roles + host tools.

6. omx_wiki vs internet search

omx_wiki MCP (wiki-server.js) stores persistent project knowledge accumulated during OMX sessions — architecture notes, decisions, glossary. It does not fetch URLs.

Recommended division:

  • Internet / official docs → Codex web_search + WebFetch (host) + optional Context7 MCP
  • Project-specific recall → omx_wiki + omx_memory

7. Code intelligence as search substitute

omx_code_intel provides LSP diagnostics and AST-grep style search within the workspace. Useful for "find pattern in repo" queries that would be wrong to send to web_search.

Contrast OMO's grep_app which searches public GitHub via remote MCP.

8. Hermes coordination MCP

omx_hermes can dispatch worker agents (including researcher role) but does not implement search itself — workers inherit host web_search availability from generated config.toml.

9. Setup path for full web research stack

Typical OMX operator configuration:

  1. omx setup / mergeConfig — preserve or set web_search = true under [features]
  2. Register Context7 MCP manually if desired (not in first-party six)
  3. Use researcher agent for docs-first external queries
  4. Use explore agent for repo-local code (not web)

10. Parity matrix

Concern co omo omx
Native web_search Yes (Rust) Via host Via host config
First-party web MCP No Exa/Tavily No
Local KB MCP memory crate — omx_wiki
Prompt-guided research — skills researcher.md
Config merge N/A OpenCode plugin mergeConfig TOML

11. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
src/config/omx-first-party-mcp.ts 15-127 Six MCP specs, manifest builder
src/config/__tests__/generator-notify.test.ts 237-259 web_search preservation
prompts/researcher.md 40-76 Web/Context7/gh guidance
src/mcp/wiki-server.ts — Local wiki MCP implementation
src/mcp/code-intel-server.ts — Local code search
CHANGELOG.md ~1779 $research → $external-context

Host lineage (not in OMX tree): 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/web_search.rs, tools/src/tool_spec.rs

Related corpus docs

  • Overview: 000_overview/00_omx_overview.md
  • Codex web search: 014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md
  • OMO Exa MCP: 014_web-search/14_omo_web-search.md
  • MCP axis: 009_mcp-orchestration/09_omx_mcp.md

12. omx_wiki MCP tools (local, not web)

wiki-server.ts exposes tools such as queryWiki, readPage, ingestKnowledge, listPages (:10-23, buildWikiServerTools :60+).

Categories: architecture, decision, pattern, debugging, environment, session-log, reference, convention (:32-41).

Not a substitute for web_search: wiki ingests session knowledge to disk under project state paths (resolveWorkingDirectoryForState :54-57). Internet facts belong in Codex host search, then optionally ingestKnowledge to wiki for retention.

13. Config generator behavior (extended)

Beyond preserving web_search = true, OMX generator adds:

  • notify hooks array
  • model_reasoning_effort
  • [agents] max_threads = 6, max_depth = 2 (generator-notify.test.ts:269-278)

None of these OMX-specific keys implement search — they shape agent parallelism for research workloads.

14. External Context7 registration

Researcher prompt references Context7 "when registered via omx setup" (researcher.md:46). Context7 is not in OMX_FIRST_PARTY_MCP_SPECS — users add it through standard Codex MCP config or setup wizard output, same as any third-party MCP.

15. When OMX is insufficient for web research

Symptom Cause Fix
Agent cannot search web web_search false/absent in config.toml Set [features] web_search = true or live per Codex docs
Researcher ignores docs Prompt-only; tools disabled Enable host search + Context7 MCP
Needs Exa like OMO No builtin Add remote Exa MCP URL to user config or use OMO on OpenCode
Needs GitHub-wide grep Only local omx_code_intel Add grep_app MCP manually or use OMO

16. Evidence quality bar (cc level checklist)

  • First-party registry exhaustive (omx-first-party-mcp.ts:15-58)
  • Generator test locks web_search preservation (generator-notify.test.ts:259)
  • Researcher prompt cites web search/fetch + Context7 (researcher.md:73-76)
  • Wiki vs web boundary from wiki-server.ts tool list
  • Explicit negative: no websearch in OMX MCP manifest builder

17. Codex WebSearchAction protocol (reference)

When web_search is enabled, Codex serializes actions matching:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/protocol/src/models.rs (lineage reference)
pub enum WebSearchAction {
    Search { query, queries },
    OpenPage { url },
    FindInPage { url, pattern },
    Other,
}

OMX agents using the researcher role may chain Search → OpenPage conceptually; OMX code does not deserialize these events — the Codex binary handles stream mapping (openai-responses-language-model in codex-rs).

18. Bootstrap MCP entrypoints

getOmxFirstPartySetupMcpServers launches each server as:

// omx-first-party-mcp.ts:90-100
command: process.execPath,
args: [join(pkgRoot, "dist", "mcp", spec.entrypoint)],
startupTimeoutSec: spec.startupTimeoutSec, // 5-10s per server

Web search latency is therefore not subject to OMX MCP startup timeouts — it uses host Responses API instead.

19. Researcher execution loop (prompt excerpt)

From researcher.md:51-60:

  1. Classify request (conceptual / implementation / history / best-practice)
  2. Find official docs or upstream source
  3. Confirm version or date context
  4. Discover doc structure before page fetches
  5. Fetch minimum targeted pages
  6. Add examples after docs baseline
  7. Use OSS references only when docs incomplete (org/repo@sha:path:Lx-Ly)

OMX encodes process; Codex web_search provides capability.

20. Comparison with Copilot CLI (cp)

Copilot CLI bundles GitHub MCP web_search fallback (151_copilot_cli/deobfuscated/.../C9t). OMX on Codex does not include GitHub MCP web search in its six servers — operators rely on OpenAI hosted search or manual MCP config.

21. Related OMX agents (non-search)

OMX ships multiple agent prompts under prompts/ — explore (repo-local), dependency-expert (package choice), researcher (external docs). Only researcher explicitly mandates web search/fetch; misrouting a web query to explore yields repo-only grep — a common operator mistake documented here for corpus parity with 003_tool-runtime routing guidance.

Corpus cross-link: OMX web axis depth intentionally documents absence of first-party search MCPs plus host delegation — same pattern as 14_lc_web-search.md (LazyCodex LSP-only) but with researcher prompt and web_search config preservation as positive evidence.

2026-06-27 WP43: first-party inventory

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current

web_search flag Generator test fixture Codex host modes independent of OMX version
Researcher prompt prompts/researcher.md Agent catalog may add/remove roles

Re-verification (WP58)

rg -n web_search /Users/jun/Developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 014_web-search/14_omx_web-search.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Oh My Codex — Web Search 구현

분석 스냅샷: 171_oh-my-codex/ — no first-party web-search MCP; internet research delegates to Codex web_search Responses tool + agent prompts; local knowledge via omx_wiki / omx_code_intel.

OMX is a Codex workflow/plugin layer. Its six first-party MCP servers cover state, memory, code intelligence, trace, wiki, and Hermes coordination — none implement HTTP crawl or search indexing. Web research is a host concern (OpenAI Codex ToolSpec::WebSearch) plus optional user MCPs configured outside OMX.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  OMX["OMX plugin / config generator"] --> Gen["mergeConfig → config.toml"]
  Gen --> WS["web_search = true (user/feature)"]
  WS --> Codex["121_openai-codex codex-rs"]
  OMX --> FP["First-party MCPs"]
  FP --> Wiki["omx_wiki — local KB"]
  FP --> CI["omx_code_intel — LSP/AST"]
  OMX --> Prompt["researcher.md agent"]
  Prompt --> Agent["Use web search/fetch + Context7"]

1. First-party MCP inventory (no web search)

  { name: "omx_memory", entrypoint: "memory-server.js", /* project memory */ },

  { name: "omx_wiki", entrypoint: "wiki-server.js", /* persistent project KB */ },

] as const;

buildOmxPluginMcpManifest() exposes these via omx mcp-serve <target> (:103-127) — no websearch target exists.

omx_wiki MCP (wiki-server.js) stores persistent project knowledge accumulated during OMX sessions — architecture notes, decisions, glossary. It does not fetch URLs.

17. Codex WebSearchAction protocol (reference)

When web_search is enabled, Codex serializes actions matching:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/protocol/src/models.rs (lineage reference)
pub enum WebSearchAction {

// omx-first-party-mcp.ts:90-100

Web search latency is therefore not subject to OMX MCP startup timeouts — it uses host Responses API instead.

  1. Classify request (conceptual / implementation / history / best-practice)
  2. Find official docs or upstream source

  4. Discover doc structure before page fetches

OMX encodes process; Codex web_search provides capability.

rg -n web_search /Users/jun/Developer/codex/171_oh-my-codex/src/

31

Reverse engineering (015)

RE

015_reverse-engineering

Overview

Reverse engineering (015) — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 이 축은 설치 help parity 문서의 다음 단계다. 기존 00~14 축은 주로 help/config/local-doc 표면을 비교했고, 이 축은 설치된 바이너리와 번들에서 복원 가능한 구조를 따로 기록한다.
  • | Level | 의미 | 사용할 수 있는 주장 |
  • | installed-help | --help, public command output, version output | 명령/플래그/공개 subcommand가 존재한다 |
  • | safe-local-doc | 설치 패키지의 docs, changelog, examples, manifest | 문서화된 schema/경로/기능 설명이 있다 |
  • | bundle-key | minified bundle에 남은 module key, source path, route name, proto name | 내부 모듈/라우터/프로토/컴포넌트가 존재한다 |
  • | binary-string | native binary strings, file, otool, codesign, nm | crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator 이름이 embedded되어 있다 |
  • | runtime-trace | synthetic project에서 실제 명령을 실행한 결과 | 동작 semantics, precedence, permission enforcement를 확인했다 |
  • | private-artifact-name | 비공개 상태 파일의 경로/개수만 관찰 | artifact가 존재한다. 내용/schema 주장은 금지 |
  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native
  • auth/token/cookie/credential/login data
  • raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB
  • logs and terminal captures

Topics covered

  • 015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md
  • Evidence Levels
  • Hard Boundary
  • Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)
  • Current Upgrade Scope
  • Reading Rule For Existing Docs

Source code snippets

171_oh-my-codex/README.md

Quick start

Requirements

  • Node.js 20+
  • Codex CLI installed, verified with codex --version, and authenticated (Homebrew or npm are both fine; do not reinstall @openai/codex with npm if Homebrew already owns codex)
  • Codex auth configured and visible in the same shell/profile that will run OMX
  • tmux on macOS/Linux if you want the recommended durable team runtime
  • psmux on native Windows only if you intentionally want the less-supported Windows team path

A good first session

After install, check both boundaries:

omx doctor
codex login status
omx exec --skip-git-repo-check -C . "Reply with exactly OMX-EXEC-OK"

omx doctor catches missing OMX files, hooks, and runtime prerequisites. The real smoke test catches auth, profile, and provider/base-URL problems that only appear when Codex performs an actual request.

Launch OMX the recommended way from a git project:

omx --worktree=feat/task --madmax --high

On macOS/Linux interactive terminals with tmux available, this starts the leader in OMX-managed detached tmux by default so the HUD/runtime panes can be created and recovered. --worktree also moves the launch into a separate git checkout, which is the safer default when using --madmax. Replace feat/task with a branch-like name for the task.

Madmax and worktree launch safety

--madmax is OMX shorthand for Codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox. It removes the normal approval and sandbox guardrails, so only use it in trusted repositories and environments. --high is shorthand for -c model_reasoning_effort="high".

When you use --madmax from a git repository, prefer a worktree launch instead of running directly in the current checkout. For repeatable or concurrent work, use a named worktree:

omx --worktree=feature/auth --madmax --high

If you are outside a git repository, omit --worktree; worktree launches require Git.

For concurrent --madmax sessions, do not run them all in the same directory. Give each session its own named worktree:

omx --worktree=feature/auth --madmax --high
omx --worktree=fix/flaky-tests --madmax --high

--worktree / -w with no name creates or reuses a detached launch worktree at ../.omx-worktrees/launch-detached. --worktree=, --worktree , or -w creates or reuses a named launch worktree under ../.omx-worktrees/ and checks out that branch name. OMX consumes the worktree flag before starting Codex; it is not forwarded to Codex itself. Treat the unnamed detached form as a one-off convenience: if the source checkout advances after that worktree is created, a later unnamed launch can fail with worktree_target_mismatch because launch-detached still points at the old HEAD. Use a named worktree for repeated work, or remove the old detached worktree before retrying. If the target launch worktree is already dirty, OMX warns and launches as-is, so clean, commit, or stash that worktree before relying on it for isolation.

For omx team, workers already use dedicated worktrees automatically by default; --worktree on omx team is only a legacy-compatible override.

If you want a one-off launch with no OMX tmux/HUD management, use --direct:

omx --direct --yolo

For a persistent shell/profile preference, set an environment policy:

OMX_LAUNCH_POLICY=direct omx --yolo

Return to the auto/default behavior with:

unset OMX_LAUNCH_POLICY

CLI policy flags win over the environment, and the last CLI policy flag before -- wins:

OMX_LAUNCH_POLICY=direct omx --tmux --yolo

Use OMX_LAUNCH_POLICY=direct|tmux|detached-tmux|auto. This iteration only adds CLI and environment controls; it intentionally does not add a config-file setting. If you run --direct from inside an existing tmux pane, OMX will not create HUD splits, enable mouse mode, or wrap extended-key handling, but the process still runs inside that already-open terminal pane.

Then try the canonical workflow:

$deep-interview "clarify the authentication change"
$ralplan "approve the safest implementation path"
$ultragoal "turn the approved path into durable Codex goals"

Use $team when an active Ultragoal story needs coordinated parallel work, or $ralph when one persistent owner should keep pushing to completion without a multi-goal ledger.

Main analysis

015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md

Reverse Engineering Evidence Policy

This axis is the next step in the installation help parity document. The existing 00 to 14 axis mainly compared the help/config/local-doc surface, and this axis separately records the structures that can be restored from installed binaries and bundles.

Evidence Levels

Level Meaning Claims available
installed-help --help, public command output, version output Command/Flag/Public subcommand exists
safe-local-doc docs, changelog, examples, manifest in the installation package There is a documented schema/path/function description
bundle-key Module key, source path, route name, proto name remaining in minified bundle Internal modules/router/prototypes/components exist
binary-string native binary strings, file, otool, codesign, nm The name crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator is embedded
runtime-trace Results of executing actual commands in synthetic project Checked operational semantics, precedence, and permission enforcement
private-artifact-name Observe only the path/count of private state files An artifact exists. Content/schema claims are prohibited

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native source-path, handler, protobuf, and runtime-trace evidence. Do not call this "full source recovered" unless a real source map or readable source archive is present.

Hard Boundary

The purpose of reverse analysis is to restore product structure, not to extract user state. Next, reading the content is prohibited.

  • auth/token/cookie/credential/login data
  • raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB
  • logs and terminal captures
  • browser profile state, local storage, IndexedDB, session storage
  • memory/goal runtime stores
  • screenshots and browser recordings
  • private mcp.json, settings, config values unless explicitly redacted

For private artifacts, only “exists” is used. Field/schema/content assertions are only used when there are public docs, source/bundle evidence, sanitized fixtures, and synthetic runtime traces.

Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)

Single source of truth for pinned ay/cr/gr/ki binaries:

  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md

WP5 trace shards (38–41) extend hubs without changing evidence-level definitions above.

Current Upgrade Scope

2026-06-27 pass — installed CLI practical RE (goal 4e94e12d-a9b):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
ay /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy v1.0.12+ help, changelog, strings; 38_ay_project_flags_trace.md
cr cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533 bundle string literals, help; 37, 39_cr_automations_help_trace.md
gr grok-0.2.67-macos-aarch64 inspect JSON, strings; 40_gr_0267_inspect_refresh.md
ki Kiro CLI.app 2.5.0 help surfaces; 41_ki_help_surface_trace.md

Newly reversed on 2026-05-31 pass (historical baseline):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
gr /Users/jun/.grok/downloads/grok-0.2.14-macos-aarch64, /Users/jun/.grok/bundled, /Users/jun/.grok/docs/user-guide Rust Mach-O strings, crate paths, ACP methods, bundled agents/roles/personas/skills
cr /Users/jun/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5 Webpack module keys, route extraction, proto module names, native helper inventory
ki /Applications/Kiro CLI.app, /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli* signed universal Mach-O binaries, Rust source-path strings, WebKit resources, dashboard/autocomplete assets
ay /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy, /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli Go/native strings, protobuf trajectory/cascade names, local cache/config metadata, helper binaries

Reading Rule For Existing Docs

When a 00~14 page says “None” or “Not supported”, interpret it as “Not exposed in installed help” unless this 15_reverse-engineering axis confirms absence from bundle/binary evidence too.

When a page states runtime semantics from config/cache observation, this axis upgrades or downgrades the claim:

  • bundle-key or binary-string confirms a module exists.
  • runtime-trace is still required for exact precedence, scheduling, isolation, merge, and permission behavior.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 015_reverse-engineering/ for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md

Reverse Engineering Evidence Policy

이 축은 설치 help parity 문서의 다음 단계다. 기존 00~14 축은 주로 help/config/local-doc 표면을 비교했고, 이 축은 설치된 바이너리와 번들에서 복원 가능한 구조를 따로 기록한다.

Evidence Levels

Level 의미 사용할 수 있는 주장
installed-help --help, public command output, version output 명령/플래그/공개 subcommand가 존재한다
safe-local-doc 설치 패키지의 docs, changelog, examples, manifest 문서화된 schema/경로/기능 설명이 있다
bundle-key minified bundle에 남은 module key, source path, route name, proto name 내부 모듈/라우터/프로토/컴포넌트가 존재한다
binary-string native binary strings, file, otool, codesign, nm crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator 이름이 embedded되어 있다
runtime-trace synthetic project에서 실제 명령을 실행한 결과 동작 semantics, precedence, permission enforcement를 확인했다
private-artifact-name 비공개 상태 파일의 경로/개수만 관찰 artifact가 존재한다. 내용/schema 주장은 금지

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native source-path, handler, protobuf, and runtime-trace evidence. Do not call this "full source recovered" unless a real source map or readable source archive is present.

Hard Boundary

역분석의 목적은 제품 구조 복원이지 사용자 상태 추출이 아니다. 다음은 내용 읽기 금지다.

  • auth/token/cookie/credential/login data
  • raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB
  • logs and terminal captures
  • browser profile state, local storage, IndexedDB, session storage
  • memory/goal runtime stores
  • screenshots and browser recordings
  • private mcp.json, settings, config values unless explicitly redacted

비공개 artifact는 “존재한다”까지만 쓴다. 필드/schema/content 주장은 public docs, source/bundle evidence, sanitized fixture, synthetic runtime trace가 있을 때만 쓴다.

Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)

Single source of truth for pinned ay/cr/gr/ki binaries:

  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md

WP5 trace shards (38–41) extend hubs without changing evidence-level definitions above.
